title: Sian Jeisel
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Sian}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Jeisel.jpg]] |name=Sian Jeisel |homeworld=[[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]]''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'' |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] {{C|16:5:23 [[Great ReSynchronization|GrS]]}}, [[Bogden 3]][[Dark Times 6|''Dark Times'' 6]] |species=[[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=*[[Color/Legends|Brown]] *White streaks |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Tan with light orange fur |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|''All'' Jedi lie, Vos. Haven't you heard? It's part of our mystique.|Sian Jeisel to Quinlan Vos|''[[Star Wars: Republic: Siege of Saleucami]]''}} '''Sian Jeisel''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A dutiful Jedi, Jeisel felt that the Order should not have gotten involved in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the major conflict between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Initially refusing to defend the Republic, Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] eventually persuaded her to return to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and take up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. After three years of fighting valiantly, Jeisel's original misgivings about the war were found to be true; the entire conflict was simply a trap for the Jedi, orchestrated by the elusive [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. The leader of both sides, Sidious, in his alter ego [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine, ordered the Republic Army to execute their Jedi leaders who had been branded as traitors to democracy. Caught unaware on [[Bogden 3]], Jeisel sacrificed herself in order to protect countless [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiates]] fleeing the carnage. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Most likely discovered at birth to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] on her homeworld of [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]], Sian Jeisel was turned over to the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Entering into the [[Jedi academy|academy]] there, Jeisel eventually went onto [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduate]] and become apprenticed to a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Passing the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] after years of study, the newly [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knighted]] Sian Jeisel continued independent study as a [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Guardian]] within the Order. ===Loyalty=== [[File:Jeiselfight.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Jeisel during the attack of Bulq's estate.]] Attaining the rank of Master after years of intensive study and work for the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Jeisel was an active member of the Order when the conflict between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] erupted into all out war. Away from Coruscant at the time of the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], Jeisel was disenchanted with the Republic, believing it too corrupt and not worthy enough for the Jedi to sacrifice their lives. Jeisel refused to answer the High Council's call to arms and instead remained abroad, gathering together with other like-minded Jedi. Initially, the High Council begrudgingly accepted this choice by a number of their ranks, but it soon became clear that if these members fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], the ensuing [[Jedi Schism|schism]] would break the Order. Summoning representatives of each breakaway group to the estate of Jedi Master [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] on the moon [[Ruul (moon)|Ruul]], Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] wished to open talks with each of the leaders and hoped to persuade them to join the war effort. Sian Jeisel responded to the summons, meeting with other dissident group leaders such as [[K'Kruhk]], [[Rhad Tarn]], and [[Mira (Near-Human)|Mira]]. While some members of the group were wary of Windu's impending visit, K'Kruhk soothed their fears as Bulq and Windu began to discuss the terms of their return. However, unbeknownst to the dissident leaders, Sora Bulq had fallen to the dark side and was in league with Confederate leader [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. When the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] attacked Bulq's estate per the [[Weequay/Legends|Weequay's]] plan, the dissident Jedi engaged her in a fight. After killing Mira, Ventress seduced Tarn to the dark side. When Tarn turned his lightsaber on Jeisel, the Devaronian [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was forced to kill him in self defense. Convinced of Bulq's turn, both Jeisel and K'Kruhk agreed to return with Master Windu to Coruscant, where they took up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. ===Siege of Saleucami=== {{Quote|I an so sorry, K'Kruhk, but there is no time to formulate new plans. The enemy is going to be at our throats soon. We need to strike first!|Sian Jeisel and K'Kruhk|}} [[File:Jeiselt5.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Sian Jeisel during the [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|Siege of Saleucami]].]] Marching onto many battlefronts, Master Jeisel was [[Mission to Drongar|en route]] to [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]] when her [[VCD987|troopship]] was boarded by Dooku's forces. When the Count himself stepped onto the bridge Jeisel, accompanied by Master [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]] and [[Kai Justiss]], refused to fall for his merciful act even as they were spared the same death as the troops under their command.''[[Jedi: Count Dooku]]'' As the war dragged on, Jeisel found herself assigned to the [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|campaign]] on [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]]. Under the command of Councilor [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]], Jeisel proudly fought [[Morgukai]] clones during the five month engagement alongside fellow Jedi Knights [[Xiaan Amersu]] and [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], as well as Masters K'Kruhk and [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]; though she butted heads with Master [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], a Jedi she, as well as many in the Order, believed had fallen to the Separatists. Wary of Vos, she suspected he had a hand in the murder of Master Rancisis half way through the battle, though he proved to be loyal as he led the Republic to victory shortly there after. ===Order 66=== {{Quote|Master Jeisel... We have to wait for her.''"
"''Jeisel will not come.|Chase Piru and K'Kruhk|}} [[File:Jeisel_order_66.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Jeisel fighting her troops during the Order 66.]] With the fires of battle dwindling on Saleucami, Jeisel and K'Kruhk were reassigned to aid Master [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] on [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]]. Taking off from the planet, their shuttle was damaged after a confrontation with a Separatist freighter and they were forced to limp to the [[Bogden Jedi chapter house|Jedi chapter house]] on [[Bogden 3]] to make repairs. As the clone troopers under their command refueled and repaired their shuttle, the two Jedi Masters were welcomed to the training facility by Master [[Du Mahn]] and the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiates]] of the [[Soaring Hawkbat Clan]]. While the Jedi and their troops relaxed in the academy's dining hall after a large feast, the clones received [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] from the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Office of the Supreme Chancellor]], an order which demanded all Jedi be killed. When her troops [[Massacre on Bogden 3|opened fire]] on the younglings, Jeisel sprung into action defending them, aiding the other Masters in cutting down the clones. After Master Mahn was killed, K'Kruhk led a contingent of surviving students out the main hall to their repaired shuttle. As K'Kruhk loaded the children on the ship, Jeisel remained in the main entrance of the facility, holding back the troopers' advance. Realizing her only way to stop the clones was to bring down the building, Jeisel engaged one trooper in hand-to-hand combat, using the Force to detonate one of his [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] and bring down the academy. Incinerated by the explosion, Jeisel's death was not in vain, as the surviving students were led to safety by her good friend Master K'Kruhk, who shed a tear over her passing. ===Legacy=== The younglings whom Jeisel saved would later escape alongside Master K'Kruhk and [[Zao]], and most lived for many years hidden from the Empire, learning the ways of the Force in peace. Reports of Master Jeisel's death inevitably reached the ears of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] on Coruscant as he kept a record of each Jedi who died. Despite this, rumors circulated across the galaxy that she indeed lived on, working as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], jumping between the stars. This rumor made its way into [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al's]] work in [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], and was treated as fact as he detailed some of the Jedi who survived the initial stages of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ==Personality and traits== [[File:JeiselSacrifice-DarkTimes6.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Jeisel's sacrifice]] {{Quote|Loyalty? I respect that.|Asajj Ventress as Jeisel leaps to defend K'Kruhk|}} Sian Jeisel was a loyal Jedi which earned her the respect of Asajj Ventress. However, Jeisel was initially disloyal to the Republic, reluctant to fight in the Clone Wars believing that the Republic was corrupt and the Jedi couldn't fight for it without corrupting themselves. Despite this, she was a skilled and brave warrior and was loyal to the Jedi Order, returning to her role in the end. Jeisel appeared to share a close friendship with K'Kruhk and saved him from Asajj Ventress during their encounter. She also highly respected Sora Bulq, before learning that he was a traitor to the Jedi Order, and believed that he was just as powerful as Mace Windu. Selfless as all good Jedi should be, Jeisel sacrificed herself during the early moments of Order 66 to allow K'Kruhk and a group of Jedi Initiates to escape from their treacherous clone troopers. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|You were always a fine Jedi Jeisel. A tough fighter and willing to take on the hard assignments.|Mace Windu|}} Sian Jeisel was a skilled warrior and her fighting prowess was respected by Mace Windu. She managed to slay the corrupt Rhad Tarn in a lightsaber duel. However, she was no match for the powerful Dark Acolyte Asajj Ventress, who was able to fend off her, Tarn, and K'Kruhk simultaneously. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Sian_as_sian.jpg|thumb|150px|The "Sian Jeisel" picture.]] Jeisel's first name was the result of a misunderstanding. [[Jan Duursema]]'s daughter, Sian, dressed as Jeisel for [[Celebration III]]. A photograph of her from that event was captioned "Sian Jeisel"; fans confused this as the character's full name, rather than the name of the person in the costume followed by what the costume portrayed. This confusion apparently led to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' using this name in text, making it official.{{JCFCite|url=posts/24223339|board=Literature|thread=When Jeisel get a first name?|user=Jan_Duursema|date=2006-06-25|time=5:10 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801162759/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/when-jeisel-get-a-first-name.24223226}} Jeisel was originally described as a Jedi Purge survivor in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', but according to [[Randy Stradley]], that information was based on a discarded story idea, and Jeisel's death in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' is now intended to be a final end for the character.{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=boards/viewtopic.php?t=12352&start=15|thread=DARK TIMES #6 -- "I don't know." *SPOILERS!*}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Sian Jeisel}} *''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Jedi: Count Dooku]]'' *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Republic 74|''Republic'' 74]] *[[Republic 75|''Republic'' 75]] *[[Republic 76|''Republic'' 76]] *[[Republic 77|''Republic'' 77]] *[[Dark Times 6|''Dark Times'' 6]] *[[Dark Times 7|''Dark Times'' 7]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' {{1stID|Sian}} *{{InsiderCite|95|Jawa's Corner}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|sorabulq|Bulq, Sora}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Sian Jeisel#legends |fr=Sian Jeisel |it=Sian Jeisel |pl=Sian Jeisel |pt=Legends:Sian Jeisel |deFA=1 |frFA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Jeisel, Sian}} [[Category:Devaronians]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Jedi Generals]] [[Category:Jedi Purge victims]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Masters]]
